figures.caption: Figure 1. Illustration of “Back to Source”. This t-SNE plot shows that while source ID (•) and OOD (•) features are wellseparated, a severe domain shift collapses their feature space, mixing ID and OOD features into an inseparable cluster (× and ×). By applying DOmain COmpensation method, the features of compensated ID (+) and OOD (+) samples are successfully realigned with the original source structure. This disentangles domain and semantic shifts, making corrupted data clearly separable again.

Figure 9. Three pathways from a corrupted image xt to a domaininvariant feature s(x): (1) pixel restoration (briefly noted), (2) training-time explicit separation (DICS), and (3) our test-time inprocess correction that learns a batch-conditioned prompt inside ϕ(·, p).

Table 11. Results (%) for LAION-C benchmark (severity = 1, κ = 0.5) in OCTTA setting across six covariate-shifted OOD datasets. All the results are averaged over 6 constantly switching domains. -L stands for applying LAION-C corruption to OOD dataset.
Table 9. Results (%) for ImageNet-to-ImageNet-C (severity = 5, κ = 0.3) in OCTTA setting across six covariate-shifted OOD datasets.

Table 3. Accuracy (%) under the closed-set setting.

Table 7. Results (%) for ImageNet-to-ImageNet-C (severity = 5, κ = 0.1) in OCTTA setting across six covariate-shifted OOD datasets.

Table 6. Results (%) for ImageNet-to-ImageNet-C benchmark (severity = 5) in OCTTA setting with different OOD samples percentages across six covariate-shifted OOD datasets. All the results are averaged over 15 domains.

Figure 3. Overview of DOCO framework. ❶ The incoming batch is processed with the prompt pt to reduce domain shift, and features zi,p are scored against source class prototypes Wh = {wc}c∈YS to split the batch into B̂ID t and B̂OOD

Figure 4. (a-b) DOCO facilitates the separation of ID and OOD by disentangling the bimodal distribution of the prototypical distance dproto(z). (c) For each new domain, the statistical loss Lstat(pt) with DOCO is consistently smaller than that of the source model on the first batch, indicating that the learned prompt generalizes well to unseen domains.
